← Back to Portfolio Residential Design — Bathroom Studies — Category 03

The smallest room, held to the highest standard.

Concept Statement

The thinking behind the room.

A collection of bathroom studies exploring the same idea from different climates and material palettes — treating the bathroom as a private spa rather than a utility. The forest-facing suite, styled with Interior Designer Charlotte Henry, pairs a dark ceiling and brass fittings with a wall of glass looking straight into the trees. The Toronto ensuite, fitted with Agua Canada fixtures, works in warm oak slatting and book-matched stone across a double vanity, walk-in shower and soaking tub. Two further studies push the material palette into deep green stone and backlit onyx, and a minimalist blackened-steel powder room shows how far the same restraint can go in a fraction of the footprint.

Designer
Niusha Meymandinejad
